metadata.s: For the present research question and PICO question, we will include RCTs only since it was the opinion of the Task Force, that these types of studies were the only relevant to address these questions.

source_row.I: Uric acid-lowering therapy (ULT; allopurinol, benzbromarone, febuxostat, oxipurinol, pegloticase, probenecid, opiroxostat, rasburicase, sulfinpyrazone, lesinurad)
source_row.O: {"Active comparator (e.g., another uric acid–lowering therapy), placebo, or usual care": ["Progression of CKD to kidney failure (follow-up: range 12 months to 84 months)", "Cutaneous reactions and hypersensitivity (follow-up: range 3 months to 41 months)", "Hepatotoxicity (follow-up: range 3 months to 25 months)"]}
source_row.P: Adults and/or children with CKD and hyperuricemia with subgroups for symptomatic and asymptomatic hyperuricemia
source_row.Question: What is the effect of uric acid–lowering therapy compared with placebo, usual care, or an active comparator among people with CKD and hyperuricemia in terms of mortality, progression of CKD, complications of CKD, and adverse events?

question_text: What is the effect of uric acid–lowering therapy compared with placebo, usual care, or an active comparator among people with CKD and hyperuricemia in terms of mortality, progression of CKD, complications of CKD, and adverse events?

C: Active comparator (e.g., another glucose-lowering agent), placebo, or usual care
I: SGLT2i (canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, ertugliflozin, ipragliflozin, luseogliflozin, remogliflozin, sotagliflozin, tofogliflozin)
O: {"Active comparator (e.g., another glucose-lowering agent), placebo, or usual care": ["Kidney failure", "All-cause hospitalizations"]}
P: Adults and/or children with CKD; subgroup of people (1) with type 2 diabetes (T2D), (2) without T2D, (3) with heart failure, and (4) without albuminuria
